chunks.text: ## Commissioner's Agenda Information Sheet
Nash County Public Schools Sale of WL Greene
Dr. Steve Ellis, Superintendent Angela Miller, Special Assistant of Auxiliary Services, Operations and Athletics

## Description:

Clerk to the Board, Janice Evans, received the following email from the Nash County Public School System regarding the sale of WL Greene.
chunks.heading: Description:

chunks.text: Clerk to the Board, Janice Evans, received the following email from the Nash County Public School System regarding the sale of WL Greene.

## Good morning Ms. Evans,

I am reaching out to see if Dr. Ellis and I could get on the agenda to ask if you would like the surplus property WL Greene.  Our board voted last night for the sale of this property, and I am reaching out to see when we could discuss in open session if the County would want this or we could sell.

252.462.2537

asmiller@ncpschools.net

## Nash County Commissioner's Agenda Information Sheet

Date: Monday, June 15, 2026

Page 1 of 2

Attachments: No

Item:

Presentation on Juneteenth Observance

Initiated By:

Tia Foula, Nash County Engagement Committee

Action Proposed:

Information Only
chunks.heading: Nash County Commissioner's Agenda Information Sheet

chunks.text: ## Nash County Commissioner's Agenda Information Sheet
Tia Foula, Nash County Engagement Committee

## Description:

The Nash County Engagement Committee invites employees and members of the Board of Commissioners to participate in a Juneteenth observance on Friday, June 19, 2026.

Juneteenth, a combination of the words "June" and "nineteenth," commemorates June 19, 1865, when Union Major General Gordon Granger arrived in Galveston, Texas, and announced General Order No. 3, informing enslaved individuals that they were free. Although President Abraham Lincoln had issued the Emancipation Proclamation more than two years earlier, enforcement of emancipation depended upon the presence of Union troops. As a result, many enslaved people in Texas remained unaware of their freedom until June 19, 1865.

Widely recognized as the nation's longest-running commemoration of the end of slavery, Juneteenth has been celebrated for generations through education, reflection, faith, cultural traditions, and community gatherings. The observance honors the resilience, perseverance, and contributions of African Americans while providing an opportunity to reflect on the ongoing pursuit of liberty, equality, and justice. In 2021, Juneteenth was officially recognized as a federal holiday.

To commemorate this important day, employees are encouraged to participate in the following activities throughout the day, as work schedules permit:

-  Explore a historical Juneteenth display located in the third-floor hallway of the County Administration Building.
-  Contribute to a collaborative community coloring wall.
-  View a Juneteenth documentary, which will be shown throughout the day in the 3rd-floor breakroom.
-  Enjoy light refreshments, puzzles, and educational activities in the breakroom.
-  Share personal reflections on the "What Freedom Means to Me" community board.
-  Support local Black-owned businesses by visiting food trucks located in the new parking lot beginning at 11:00 a.m.

Employees are encouraged to wear Juneteenth-themed attire or commemorative colors in recognition o
chunks.heading: Description:

chunks.text: The Nash County Engagement Committee invites employees and members of the Board of Commissioners to participate in a Juneteenth observance on Friday, June 19, 2026.

Juneteenth, a combination of the words "June" and "nineteenth," commemorates June 19, 1865, when Union Major General Gordon Granger arrived in Galveston, Texas, and announced General Order No. 3, informing enslaved individuals that they were free. Although President Abraham Lincoln had issued the Emancipation Proclamation more than two years earlier, enforcement of emancipation depended upon the presence of Union troops. As a result, many enslaved people in Texas remained unaware of their freedom until June 19, 1865.

Widely recognized as the nation's longest-running commemoration of the end of slavery, Juneteenth has been celebrated for generations through education, reflection, faith, cultural traditions, and community gatherings. The observance honors the resilience, perseverance, and contributions of African Americans while providing an opportunity to reflect on the ongoing pursuit of liberty, equality, and justice. In 2021, Juneteenth was officially recognized as a federal holiday.
To commemorate this important day, employees are encouraged to participate in the following activities throughout the day, as work schedules permit:

-  Explore a historical Juneteenth display located in the third-floor hallway of the County Administration Building.
-  Contribute to a collaborative community coloring wall.
-  View a Juneteenth documentary, which will be shown throughout the day in the 3rd-floor breakroom.
-  Enjoy light refreshments, puzzles, and educational activities in the breakroom.
-  Share personal reflections on the "What Freedom Means to Me" community board.
-  Support local Black-owned businesses by visiting food trucks located in the new parking lot beginning at 11:00 a.m.
Employees are encouraged to wear Juneteenth-themed attire or commemorative colors in recognition of the observance.

This event provides an opportunity for learning, reflection, and community engagement as Nash County recognizes an important chapter in American history and honors the enduring values of freedom, opportunity, and service to others.
This version reads more like a commissioner agenda abstract and emphasizes both the historical significance of Juneteenth and the educational purpose of the County's observance.

Nash County Legislative Goals

Initiated By:

Emily Moore, Grants & Intergovernmental Relations Director

Action Proposed:

Adopt Legislative Goals

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
chunks.heading: Commissioner's Agenda Information Sheet

chunks.text: ## Commissioner's Agenda Information Sheet
Emily Moore, Grants & Intergovernmental Relations Director

## Description:

The  North  Carolina  Association  of  County  Commissioners  (NCACC)  is  currently involved  in  its  2026-2027  Legislative  Goals  Process.  Every  two  years,  in  the  months preceding a long session of the General Assembly, the NCACC engages counties in this  process,  which  enables  county  commissioners  across  the  State  to  reach  a consensus on legislative priorities and speak with one voice  through the NCACC. To develop its goals, the NCACC is presently soliciting input from all 100 counties.

Attached in Exhibit A is a list of possible legislative priorities from Nash County. All the goals were discussed at the 2026 Nash County Legislative Dinner.

Following  approval  by  the  County  Commissioners,  we  will  submit  a  copy  of  the Resolution and Exhibit A (the Legislative Goals) to the NCACC. We'll also submit the required  separate  'legislative  goals  proposal  form'  online  for  each  priority  by  the September goals submission deadline.
chunks.heading: Description:

chunks.text: The  North  Carolina  Association  of  County  Commissioners  (NCACC)  is  currently involved  in  its  2026-2027  Legislative  Goals  Process.  Every  two  years,  in  the  months preceding a long session of the General Assembly, the NCACC engages counties in this  process,  which  enables  county  commissioners  across  the  State  to  reach  a consensus on legislative priorities and speak with one voice  through the NCACC. To develop its goals, the NCACC is presently soliciting input from all 100 counties.
Attached in Exhibit A is a list of possible legislative priorities from Nash County. All the goals were discussed at the 2026 Nash County Legislative Dinner.

Following  approval  by  the  County  Commissioners,  we  will  submit  a  copy  of  the Resolution and Exhibit A (the Legislative Goals) to the NCACC. We'll also submit the required  separate  'legislative  goals  proposal  form'  online  for  each  priority  by  the September goals submission deadline.

## Recommended Action:

We are recommending that the Board adopt the attached Resolution and approve its submission to the North Carolina Association of County Commissioners.

chunks.text: We are recommending that the Board adopt the attached Resolution and approve its submission to the North Carolina Association of County Commissioners.
